hero.question: LoL: Top Esports vs JD Gaming - Game 2 Winner
hero.prob_take: The crowd has essentially decided Top Esports takes Game 2 at 93%, pricing in their superior mechanics and map control in a matchup where JD Gaming needs a perfect read to steal a map. That's fair value or maybe even slightly generous to Top given JD's late-game teamfighting, but at 93% you're getting paid peanuts to eat potential variance.

daily_take.headline: Top Esports Priced Like a Sure Thing. The Market Is Sweating.
daily_take.deck: Top Esports hit 93% overnight on game 2 odds, a 47-point rip in 24 hours. That's not confidence, that's panic buying. The question: did sharp money figure out something about the matchup, or did retail pile in because game 1 looked convincing? At 93%, you're getting paid 7-to-1 for a team that still has to execute. The play is clear: either take the contrarian 7% or wait for a live bleed if Top Esports looks vulnerable early.

sidebar.headline: Belmont's Peru surge has real juice. Five-point pop on political momentum, but 27% still undervalues a fragmented field.
sidebar.label: Ricardo Belmont: 27.2%

sidebar.headline: McIlroy's Masters bid just became real money. 46-point spike signals pros repositioning. 70% is still the right price if he stays healthy.
sidebar.label: Rory McIlroy 2026 Masters: 70.5%

sidebar.headline: Hormuz normalcy by April is a long shot that's getting longer. Four-point move on Middle East jitters. 24% doesn't sound crazy but feels generous given regional escalation risk.
sidebar.label: Strait of Hormuz Normal by April: 24.5%
